message.content: Adversaries may exploit MSBuild, a trusted Windows utility, to execute malicious code by embedding it within XML project files. MSBuild, part of the Microsoft Build Engine, compiles and runs these files, which can include C# or Visual Basic code. This technique leverages MSBuild's signed binary status to bypass application control defenses. Commonly, threat actors use this method to execute arbitrary code stealthily, as seen in campaigns like Frankenstein and with malware such as PlugX. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and restricting the use of MSBuild, implementing strict application whitelisting, and employing behavioral analysis to detect unusual MSBuild activity. Regularly updating security policies to include checks for misuse of trusted utilities is also crucial.
